Added tests/shm.rs consumer_death_holding_msg_redelivers_at_least_once (at-least-once redelivery of an in-flight Msg after recovery) and shm.rs unit tests odd_generation_is_rejected_as_initializing + even_generation_attaches (seqlock generation guard). The true concurrent create/attach RACE is inherently non-deterministic; the deterministic guard paths are now covered.

Done: native-endian/same-host note added to shm module doc (+ fixed layout-table ordering), ShmItem usage example added, README links examples/ipc_pair.rs. AnchorKind was already fully documented (review item moot); 'from_le vs from_le_bytes' confirmed not a real inconsistency.
Descoped at user request: the cpp-fastchan lineage isn't relevant to rust-rb usage. Guide removed (docs/guide/migration.md, guide::migration module, crate-hub link).

Implemented in full on branch spmc-design: spmc + spmc_bytes (gating) and broadcast + broadcast_bytes (lossy), heap + shm (PROT_READ lossy consumers, zombie-retirement gating recovery), SleepWait/BackoffWait/SelfTimed, benches with pre-registered gates, docs. Every commit Miri-gated + adversarially audited; one real data race found & fixed pre-merge (M-F2 bitmap ordering). Perf findings tracked separately (rust-rb-vio, rust-rb-6l0). 250 tests.
